expected_trades_per_day_basis: Reference E148 pc_pump_global_short_high_long fired 988 times (~33/day across 30d); this archetype adds two simultaneous filters (funding>=0.0005 AND taker_buy_ratio_5m<=0.40) on top of the pump>=3% gate, each cutting ~70-80%, yielding ~50-60 events / 30 days ≈ 1.8/day.

best_in: BTC choppy/range days with elevated funding (>0.05% 8h) on alts
post-news euphoric pump bars where price spikes >3% but spot CVD/taker-buy ratio stays <0.40 (futures-led, no spot follow-through)
crowded-long phases identified by funding top-decile across the futures board
worst_in: Strong BTC trend-up days where alt pumps continue for 30-60m past entry
low-funding regimes (funding<0.01%) where the crowded-long premise is absent — signal degrades to a pure momentum fade
exchange-listing / unlock catalyst pumps where flow is one-directional despite negative tape internals
regime_sensitivity.explanation: The edge is structural: positive funding + taker-sell dominance during a pump means longs are paying to hold while aggressive flow is exiting, a textbook reversion setup. It fails when the pump is genuinely catalyst-driven, because internals lag price and the fade gets run over.

live_readiness_caveats: Funding rate must be sampled at entry from the SAME timestamp the bar closes — using a stale 8h-old funding snapshot will misclassify ~25% of triggers and is the most likely live/backtest divergence.

taker_buy_ratio_5m is computed on aggregated trades; on low-volume symbols (<$5M 5m volume) this metric is noisy and can flip sign within seconds — restrict to symbols with >$10M 5m turnover to keep the filter meaningful.

Symmetric TP/SL geometry only protects expectancy if slippage on entry is <0.10% — short entries during a vertical pump bar often fill 0.2-0.5% worse than mark, which converts a +0.4 lift into break-even.

monitoring_metrics.rationale: Either filter is misimplemented (too loose → trap-shaped distribution returns) or funding/taker-buy regime has shifted enough to invalidate the premise.
This is a mean-reversion short; median (not mean) is the cleanest read on whether the typical fade is still working — mean is dominated by the rare runaway pump losses.
Fades into vertical bars are slippage-prone; if p75 slippage exceeds 15 bps the symmetric TP/SL geometry no longer holds and edge collapses.
If a quarter of triggers come in below the intended funding floor, the filter is admitting non-crowded setups and the archetype is drifting toward a generic momentum short.
At ~1.8 trades/day with TP≈SL geometry expected win rate ~55-60%; consecutive losses >6 indicates regime breakdown (trending pumps), not random variance.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling Sharpe < 0 AND median_trade_pct < 0 simultaneously for 5 trading days
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Both central-tendency and risk-adjusted metrics negative rules out variance — the mean-reversion premise is no longer present in the current market.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Realized max drawdown on paper account exceeds 8% within first 30 days
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Backtest expectation is ~5% paper-window DD; exceeding 8% means tail risk is materially worse than backtest, almost certainly from execution gap on vertical-bar entries.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Three consecutive trades where funding flipped negative within 15m of entry
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Funding regime change in real time means the crowded-long thesis evaporated mid-trade; this is the single observable kill-signal that the structural edge has disappeared.

expected_max_dd_basis: Symmetric TP=SL geometry with backtest win rate ~55-60% and ~1.8 trades/day implies a worst-case streak of 4-5 losses per 30d window, ≈ 5×1.0% per-trade loss on 5-10% sizing ≈ 5% account DD.
compare_to_existing: Closest live/top peer is E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long inverted — same pricechange channel, same taker-flow construct, but flipped sign and side; this archetype should mirror E126's frequency profile (~3k/30d before filters) shrunk by funding+ratio gates, and should specifically NOT exhibit the asymmetric TP/SL trap that made E126's 0.3946 backtest compound to -13.5% paper, because TP=SL by construction here.
